title: Tables
description: For tabular data — never for layout.

questions.prompt: Which element wraps a HEADER row's cells?
questions.explanation: Header cells are <th>. Group them inside <thead> for semantics.

options: Adds an image below the table
Provides an accessible title/description for the table
questions.explanation: <caption> is the accessible title of the table. Screen readers announce it.

questions.prompt: Which attribute makes a cell span 2 columns?
Semantic wrappers for body rows and footer rows:
questions.explanation: thead, tbody, tfoot group rows for a11y and styling.

options: Considered bad practice; hurts a11y and mobile
Only OK in emails (email HTML uses tables)
questions.explanation: Web pages: use grid/flex. Email HTML historically requires tables for compatibility.

questions.prompt: Which attribute on <th> improves a11y for cross-referenced data?
questions.explanation: <th scope="col"> or scope="row" ties header cells to data cells.
All are technically valid; just be intentional. Nested tables are usually a smell though.

questions.prompt: Which CSS property collapses table borders into single lines?
questions.explanation: border-collapse: collapse merges adjacent cell borders.
